visits.note: Patient P096 is a 55-year-old male presenting with a 5-year history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. His blood pressure is elevated at 140/90 mmHg and HbA1c is 7.2%. He is prescribed Metoprolol 50mg daily and Simvastatin 20mg daily. Diagnoses include Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, and Type 2 Diabetes. Symptoms include fatigue and polyuria.

Patient P096 returns with worsening hypertension at 150/95 mmHg and increasing fatigue. His HbA1c has improved slightly to 6.8%. He is added to Lisinopril 10mg daily for hypertension control. New symptoms include dyspnea. Diagnoses now include Chronic Kidney Disease due to worsening eGFR.

Patient P096's hypertension remains uncontrolled at 160/100 mmHg. His HbA1c has worsened to 7.5%. Due to worsening kidney function, he is started on Furosemide 40mg daily for edema management. New symptoms include orthopnea.

Patient P096's kidney function has continued to decline, with an eGFR of 45. His hypertension remains uncontrolled at 155/105 mmHg. Due to worsening cardiovascular risk, he is started on Aspirin 81mg daily for antiplatelet therapy. New symptoms include paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea.
